title: Nim Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Nim source files, including both regular modules (`.nim`) and NimScript configuration files (`.nims`). Nim compiles to C, C++, or JavaScript and emphasizes performance and expressiveness.

## When to use
Use `media:nim;textable;code` when a pipeline step consumes or produces Nim source — for example, a transpilation node targeting C output or a static analysis tool operating on Nim modules.

## Notes
NimScript (`.nims`) files share the same syntax as Nim but run in a sandboxed interpreter used for build configuration. Both extensions are covered by this media type. See also [Nim Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:nim;textable;code).
